sowingSchedule.frostNote: Last spring frost locally falls in the first half of May. Heated gloves stay by the door until then.
sowingSchedule.method: Started under shop lights in the greenhouse on the heat mat.
Heat mat only, moved to lights after germination.
Sown straight into the bed once soil can be worked.

packet.transplant: Out after the last frost, hardened off over a week.
packet.notes: Slow from seed, which is why they go in first. Cool germination, do not overwater.

packet.transplant: To the part-shade border once settled.
packet.notes: Surface sow, needs light to germinate. Biennial habit, plan for next year too.

packet.transplant: First flat to the cold frame in early spring, successions to follow.
packet.notes: Quick and forgiving. Gentle on the stomach, which matters here.

packet.transplant: Edge of the sunny border, will self-seed thereafter.
packet.notes: Cheerful, tough, good company for the vegetable beds.

packet.transplant: To Harriet's raised beds well after the soil warms.
packet.notes: Harriet's bed, my advice only. Needs real warmth, no rush.

packet.transplant: Harriet decides the varieties and the date. The tomato question is hers.
packet.notes: I miss eating these more than anything. Advisory staff only on the tomato bed.

packet.transplant: Planted in fall, mulched deep, lifted the following summer.
packet.notes: Went in last autumn before the ground hardened. Strong shoots already up under the straw.

standingRules.rule: Right plant, right place. Resist adding a third of anything.
Label every flat the day it is sown. The garden remembers what I forget, but only if I write it down.
Water new transplants through their first dry spell, then let them earn their keep.
